title: Compute Whole Number Division as Fraction
description: Divide a whole number by a whole number and express the result as a fraction.
condition: Expression of the form a ÷ b where a and b are whole numbers and the quotient is not a whole number.
response: Write the result as the fraction a/b.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on understanding division as forming a fraction.

title: Convert Improper Fraction to Mixed Number
description: Convert an improper fraction to an equivalent mixed number by dividing the numerator by the denominator.
condition: Improper fraction a/b where a > b.
response: Divide a by b to get whole number q and remainder r; write q r/b.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Mixed Number to Improper Fraction
description: Rewrite a mixed number as an improper fraction before multiplication.
condition: A mixed number (e.g., $a \frac{b}{c}$) appears as a factor in multiplication.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Alternative strategy is distributive property (see separate KC).

title: Count Equal Fractional Parts for Area
description: Find total area by counting equal shaded parts and multiplying by the unit fraction each part represents.
condition: Region partitioned into equal fractional parts with some number shaded.
response: Multiply number of shaded parts by the fraction represented by each part.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 8 parts are shaded and each is $\frac{1}{5}$ square unit, area $= 8 \times \frac{1}{5} = \frac{8}{5}$.

title: Interpret Division as Equal Shares
description: Interpret a ÷ b as dividing a whole into b equal parts.
condition: Expression of the form a ÷ b with whole numbers.
response: Represent as a wholes partitioned into b equal shares each.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $3 \div 5$ means 3 wholes split into 5 equal parts each.

title: Interpret Fraction as Division
description: Explain that a fraction a/b represents the division of a by b.
condition: A fraction written in the form a/b.
response: State that it represents a ÷ b and justify using equal shares or partitioning.
label: Principle / Model
example: $\frac{10}{4}$ means $10 \div 4$, or 10 items split into 4 equal groups.
notes: Central conceptual link between fractions and division.

title: Interpret Fraction as Equal Parts of a Whole
description: Recognize that 1/n represents one of n equal parts of a whole.
response: State that it represents one of n equal parts of a whole.
label: Concept

title: Interpret Rectangle Area as Product of Side Lengths
description: Represent the area of a rectangle as the product of its side lengths, including fractional side lengths.
condition: Rectangle with side lengths given (whole and/or fractional).
response: Multiply the side lengths to compute area.
label: Principle / Model
example: A rectangle 4 units by $\frac{2}{5}$ units has area $4 \times \frac{2}{5} = \frac{8}{5}$.

title: Interpret Tape Diagram for Division
description: Identify the total number of wholes and the number of equal partitions in a tape diagram.
condition: A tape diagram showing several whole units, each partitioned into equal parts.
response: Determine the total number of wholes (dividend) and the number of equal parts per whole (divisor).
label: Production / Skill
example: A diagram with 4 wholes each split into 3 equal parts represents 4 ÷ 3.
notes: Requires visual parsing of diagram structure.

title: Multiply Whole Number by Fraction
description: Multiply a whole number by a fraction by multiplying the whole number by the numerator and dividing by the denominator.
condition: Expression of the form N \u00d7 a/b.
response: Compute (N \u00d7 a)/b and simplify if needed.
label: Principle / Model

title: Represent Equal Groups with Multiplication
description: Express repeated equal groups as a multiplication expression.
condition: Situation describing g equal groups of size s.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Connects visual groups to symbolic multiplication.

title: Rewrite Mixed Number as Whole Plus Fraction
description: Decompose a mixed number into the sum of its whole-number part and fractional part.
condition: Mixed number of the form w a/b.
label: Principle / Model

title: Select Expression Equivalent to Area Representation
description: Determine which numerical expressions are equivalent to a given area model or context.
condition: Multiple candidate expressions representing the same shaded area or situation.
response: Identify all expressions that evaluate to the same quantity as the model.
label: Principle / Model
example: If area is $\frac{11}{4} \times 5$, equivalent forms include $(5 \times 3) + (5 \times \frac{2}{5})$ when they evaluate to the same total.

title: Simplify or Rewrite Fraction Result
description: Rewrite an improper fraction as a mixed number or simplify to an equivalent fraction.
condition: A fraction result that can be simplified or written as a mixed number.
response: Divide numerator by denominator to form a mixed number or reduce by common factors.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Equivalent answers (improper fraction or mixed number) are acceptable.

title: Translate Area Model to Numerical Expression
description: Match shaded equal parts in a diagram to a corresponding multiplication or division expression.
condition: Area model with wholes partitioned into equal parts and some parts shaded.
response: Write an expression (e.g., a ÷ b, a × 1/n, or g × b/c) that represents the shaded quantity.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Requires coordinating visual partitions with symbolic structure.

title: Use Distributive Property with Fractions
description: Apply the distributive property to multiply a whole number by a mixed number or sum involving a fraction.
condition: Expression of the form N \u00d7 (a + b) where a or b may be fractional.
label: Principle / Model

title: Write Division Equation from Diagram
description: Construct a matching division equation based on a visual model.
condition: A visual model (e.g., tape diagram) showing a total number of wholes partitioned equally.
response: Write the corresponding division equation dividend ÷ divisor = quotient.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 4 wholes are each divided into 3 equal parts, write $4 \div 3 = \frac{4}{3}$.
notes: Builds on correctly identifying diagram components.
